content: User is creating a framework for generating fantasy world characters with parameters including attributes, descriptions, and motives, using curly brackets for proper nouns.
evidence_span: we are going to create a framework for generating characters belonging to a fantasy world with parameters which can include but is not limited to: attributes, descriptions, and motives. curly brackets indicate proper nouns and should be tagged for future use as we expand the world (e.g. {proper noun}).
assistant_replies:
[Name = Elara Moonwhisper]
[Background = Enchanter]
[Description = A woman in her mid-twenties with silver hair and amethyst colored eyes, often wears a flowing lavender robe]
[Personality = quiet and reserved, often lost in thought, possesses a love for nature and all living things]

{Elara Moonwhisper} is on a mission to retrieve a powerful enchanted flower from the {Shadowrealm} that can heal any wound. She is seeking brave adventurers to accompany her on this dangerous journey as the realm is inhabited by fearsome {Shadow creatures}.

content: User provided an example character: Rishi Swiftwind, a hunter with jet black hair and emerald eyes, quick-tempered, speaks with a stutter, prefers to be alone, recovering from an expedition to Dreadhollow Forest where he suspects wolves were Werewolves.
evidence_span: [name = Rishi Swiftwind] [background = hunter] [description = a man in his early thirties with jet black hair and emerald colored eyes] [personality = quick-tempered, speaks with a sutter, prefers to be alone] {Rishi Swiftwind} is currently recovering from an expedition to the {Dreadhollow Forest} where he sustained an injury hunting a pack of wolves he suspects to be secretly {Werewolves}.

content: User requested characters to be more normal people with simple occupations and tasks, not world-ending disasters.

dimension.state_value: normal people, simple occupations, simple tasks

evidence_span: Alright, can we make the characters more normal people? I want these characters to have simple occupations and relatively simple tasks as opposed to world ending disaster for the party to help out with
assistant_replies:
Of course, here's an updated example:

[Name = Jacob Blacksmith]
[Background = Blacksmith]
[Description = A man in his forties with a sturdy build and calloused hands, wears a leather apron over his simple tunic and pants]
[Personality = friendly, hardworking, enjoys a good ale at the local tavern]

{Jacob Blacksmith} is in need of rare materials to forge a custom order for a wealthy client. He is seeking adventurers to help gather these materials from the nearby {Iron Mountains}.

[Name = Ava Greenkeeper]
[Background = Gardener]
[Description = A woman in her late thirties with a kind smile and weathered hands, often wears a wide brimmed hat to protect herself from the sun]
[Personality = gentle, nurturing, has a love for all growing things]

{Ava Greenkeeper} is looking for assistance in replanting a grove of rare trees in the {Elvendom Forest}. She is searching for a group of reliable individuals to help transport the saplings and protect them from any potential threats in the forest.

content: User requested one character at a time and criticized the name 'Jacob Blacksmith' as too on the nose, asking to change it.

relation.subject: user
relation.object: name Jacob Blacksmith as too on the nose

evidence_span: Just give me one character at a time. Also jacob blacksmith is a weird name for a blacksmith -- seems too on the nose. let's change that.
assistant_replies:
Understood. Here's an updated character:

[Name = Markus Ironfist]
[Background = Blacksmith]
[Description = A rugged man in his forties with broad shoulders and a bushy beard, wears a leather apron over his soot-stained tunic and pants]
[Personality = gruff, skilled with his hands, values hard work and fairness]

{Markus Ironfist} is in need of rare metals to forge a custom order for a wealthy client. He is seeking adventurers to help gather these metals from the nearby {Dragon's Teeth Mines}.
